hypothesis: When predicting etching rates for three-or-more component gas mixtures using the GraphSAGE framework, edge features encoding pairwise chemical interaction parameters (such as reaction rate constants or ionization cross-sections between species pairs) will yield significantly higher predictive accuracy than node-feature-only representations, because the increased combinatorial complexity of multi-component plasma chemistry encodes interaction information that cannot be adequately captured by aggregating individual single-gas embeddings alone.
